title: 10 Herbal Teas That Ease Palpitation Naturally
intro_desc: Herbal teas for palpitation are a natural remedy that helps alleviate irregular heartbeats and anxiety-induced palpitations. These teas work by calming the nervous system, regulating heart rate, and reducing stress. The best herbal teas for palpitation include Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, Lavandula angustifolia, Avena sativa, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Zingiber officinale, Ziziphus jujuba, Leonurus cardiaca, Centella asiatica, and Scutellaria baicalensis. If left untreated, palpitations can lead to sleep disturbances, fatigue, and decreased productivity, negatively impacting daily life and overall well-being.

remedies.remedy_desc: Valeriana officinalis tea is a natural remedy for palpitations. Palpitations are irregular heartbeats that can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other medical conditions. The tea is made from the root of the valerian plant, which has been used for centuries to calm the nervous system and promote relaxation. When consumed, the tea helps to slow down heart rate and reduce anxiety levels, making it easier to manage palpitations. The active compounds in valeriana officinalis, such as valerenic acid and isovaleric acid, work together to calm the heart and promote a sense of calmness. As a result, the tea can help to reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations, making it a popular natural remedy for people who experience irregular heartbeats. Additionally, valeriana officinalis tea has been shown to improve sleep quality, which can also help to reduce palpitations. Drinking a cup of valeriana officinalis tea before bedtime can help to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety levels, making it easier to fall asleep and stay asleep.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Valerenic acid is good for palpitations because it has a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to slow down heart rate and reduce anxiety that can cause rapid or irregular heartbeats.
Valeranone is good for palpitation because it has a calming effect on the heart, helping to slow down rapid heartbeat and reduce stress and anxiety.

Valerenal is good for palpitations because it can help to calm the nervous system, reduce anxiety and stress, and improve sleep quality, which in turn can slow down the heart rate and reduce irregular heartbeat.
Isovaleric acid is good for palpitations because it helps to reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, thereby improving heart function and regulating heart rhythm.
Tannins (St. John's Wort) are good for palpitations because they help relax the heart muscles and improve blood flow, which reduces anxiety and stress that can cause irregular heartbeat.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum tea, also known as St John's Wort tea, start by gathering high-quality dried Hypericum perforatum flowers. Begin by boiling a cup of water and then letting it cool for a minute or two. Next, take one teaspoon of the dried flowers and add it to the cooled water. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, so the herbs can release their active ingredients. After the steeping time has passed, strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids. You can discard the solids or save them for future use. Once you've strained the tea, you can add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. It's recommended to drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate palpitations and other symptoms associated with anxiety and stress. However, please consult with a healthcare professional before using Hypericum perforatum tea, especially if you're taking any medications or have underlying health conditions.

remedies.remedy_desc: Passiflora incarnata tea, also known as passionflower tea, has been used for centuries to help people with palpitations. Palpitations are irregular heartbeats that can be caused by stress, anxiety, or other health issues. The tea is made from the flowers of the Passiflora incarnata plant, which is rich in chemicals that can help calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. When we're anxious or stressed, our heart beats faster and can become irregular. Passionflower tea can help slow down heart rate and promote a sense of calmness, making it easier to breathe and relax. It's also known to have a sedative effect, which can help reduce the frequency and severity of palpitations. By reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, passionflower tea can help people with palpitations feel more calm and in control. The tea is also thought to help regulate the body's natural sleep-wake cycle, which can be disrupted in people with palpitations. Regular consumption of passionflower tea may help alleviate palpitations and promote a sense of well-being.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea that helps with palpitations, start by gathering dried St. John's Wort flowers and leaves. Take one teaspoon of the dried herb and place it in a cup or teapot. Next, boil fresh water in a kettle or pot, then pour it over the herb. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and medicinal properties to infuse into the water. After the steeping time, strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into another cup, discarding the solids. Your Hypericum perforatum tea is now ready to drink. As with any herbal remedy, consult with a healthcare professional before using it, especially if you're already taking medication or have a pre-existing condition. To help with palpitations, drink one to two cups of the tea a day, ideally after meals, and observe how your body responds.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginger tea, made from Zingiber officinale, can help with palpitations because it has natural properties that calm the heart. When you drink ginger tea, the active ingredients like gingerol and shogaol go into your body and help relax the blood vessels. This relaxation helps slow down the heart rate and reduce the force of the heartbeat, which in turn reduces palpitations. Ginger tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the heart and surrounding blood vessels. This can also help lower blood pressure, another common cause of palpitations. Additionally, ginger tea has been shown to improve digestion, which can help prevent irregular heartbeats caused by an empty stomach or digestive issues. By soothing the digestive system and relaxing the blood vessels, ginger tea can provide relief from palpitations and promote a more regular and healthy heartbeat.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Hypericum perforatum herbal tea that helps with palpitations, start by gathering fresh or dried flowers of the St John's Wort plant. If using fresh flowers, pick them in the morning after the dew has evaporated, and clean them thoroughly. For dried flowers, make sure they are free of contaminants. Next, measure one teaspoon of the flowers for every cup of boiling water you plan to use, and place them in a teapot or infuser. Pour boiling water over the flowers, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the active ingredients to release into the water. Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the flowers, and add honey or lemon to taste if desired. Drink 1-2 cups of the tea per day, ideally after meals, to help manage palpitations caused by stress, anxiety, or other factors. supplementary_best: A combination of Valeriana officinalis, Passiflora incarnata, and Lavandula angustifolia can help remedy palpitations. Valeriana officinalis, also known as valerian root, is known for its calming effects and ability to lower anxiety levels, which can contribute to palpitations. Passiflora incarnata, also known as passionflower, is a natural sedative that can help calm the nervous system and reduce stress. Lavandula angustifolia, or lavender, is known for its ability to promote relaxation and reduce anxiety. This combination works well together because Valeriana officinalis and Passiflora incarnata can help lower anxiety levels and promote relaxation, while Lavandula angustifolia can enhance these effects and promote a sense of calm. This combination is particularly effective because it targets multiple aspects of palpitations, including anxiety and stress, making it a well-rounded remedy. The valerian root helps regulate the nervous system, the passionflower calms the mind, and the lavender soothes the body, creating a powerful synergy that can help alleviate palpitations.
supplementary_avoid: If you have palpitations, it's best to avoid certain herbal teas that can worsen the condition. For example, tea made from Yerba Mate (Ilex paraguariensis) should be avoided as it contains caffeine, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure, making palpitations worse. Tea made from Ephedra (Ephedra sinica) is also not recommended, as it contains ephedrine, a stimulant that can raise blood pressure and heart rate. Yohimbe (Pausinystalia johimbe) tea is another one to avoid, as it can increase heart rate and blood pressure due to its stimulant properties. Peppermint tea (Mentha piperita) is generally safe but should be consumed in moderation, as excessive intake can cause heart palpitations in some individuals. Finally, tea made from Guarana (Paullinia cupana) should be avoided due to its high caffeine content, which can exacerbate palpitations.
supplementary_other_remedies: Ginger has been used for centuries to help calm palpitations. Its active compounds, gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation in the body, which can contribute to palpitations. When consumed as a juice or in food, ginger can also help relax the muscles in the heart and improve blood flow, making it easier for the heart to beat regularly. The warm and soothing effects of ginger can also help calm the nervous system, which can contribute to palpitations caused by anxiety or stress.  Other natural remedies that can help with palpitations include ashwagandha, an herb that helps reduce stress and anxiety by regulating the body's response to stress. When consumed as a powder or supplement, ashwagandha can help calm the nervous system and improve sleep quality, both of which can contribute to palpitations. Apple cider vinegar, which contains acetic acid, can also help regulate heart rhythm and improve blood flow by dilating blood vessels. The potassium content in apple cider vinegar can also help counteract the effects of sodium, which can contribute to palpitations.  Aromatherapy using essential oils such as lavender and chamomile can also help calm palpitations by promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety. When inhaled, these oils can help slow down heart rate and promote a sense of calm, making it easier for the heart to beat regularly. Additionally, magnesium, a mineral that plays a crucial role in heart function, can help regulate heart rhythm and reduce palpitations when consumed as a supplement or applied topically. The relaxing effects of magnesium can also help reduce muscle tension and improve sleep quality, both of which can contribute to palpitations.
supplementary_related_ailments: People who experience palpitations often suffer from other health issues, such as high blood pressure, anxiety, and insomnia. Palpitations can be caused by stress, which is a common trigger for anxiety. To manage these conditions, medicinal herbs like ashwagandha, passionflower, and valerian root can be helpful. Ashwagandha, an adaptogenic herb, helps reduce stress levels by balancing the body's response to it. Passionflower, a calming herb, soothes the nervous system and reduces anxiety. Valerian root, a natural sedative, promotes relaxation and improves sleep quality. These herbs can also help lower blood pressure by reducing inflammation and improving circulation. Additionally, people with palpitations may also experience digestive issues like irritable bowel syndrome (IBS) due to stress. Herbs like peppermint and ginger can help alleviate IBS symptoms by calming the digestive system and reducing inflammation. Another common issue is anemia, which can cause palpitations due to low iron levels. Iron-rich herbs like dandelion and nettle leaf can help boost iron levels and alleviate palpitations.
